content:
MODULE: cosmos.network.cosmos_node
FILE: cosmos/network/cosmos_node.py (1409 lines)
CATEGORY: network

DESCRIPTION: COSMOS Node Daemon
==================

The heart of the COSMOS mesh. Each server runs one node that:
  - Listens on TCP port 9999 (TCP_NODELAY for speed)
  - Manages connections to peer nodes (persistent, multiplexed)
  - Routes DIALOGUE requests to local bots (Ollama, API agents)
  - Streams respon

CLASSES:
- PeerConnection: Active connection to a peer node.
- COSMOSNode: COSMOS mesh node daemon.

Handles:
- Incoming peer connections (server mode)
- O
  Methods: def register_bot(self, bot_name: str, query_fn: Callable)
  Methods: def get_local_bots(self) -> List[str]
  Methods: def get_all_bots(self) -> Dict[str, str]
  Methods: async def start(self)
  Methods: async def stop(self)
  Methods: async def consensus_query(self, prompt: str, model_name: str, min_validators: int=2, timeout: float=
  Methods: async def latent_query(self, prompt: str, max_tokens: int=4000) -> Optional[Tuple[str, Dict]]
  Methods: async def propose_memory(self, content: str, source_prompt: str, source_model: str, tags: Optional[L

PUBLIC FUNCTIONS:
- def get_cosmos_node() -> Optional[COSMOSNode]
- async def start_cosmos_node(node_name: str, host: str='0.0.0.0', port: int=COSMOS_PORT) -> COSMOSNode

INTERNAL DEPS: cosmos_mesh, cosmos_config, cosmos_swarm_memory, cosmos_attestation, cosmos_protocol, cosmos_latent_router, cosmos_auth, cosmos_poi
